channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Student Culinary Council Meeting - March 18th
item.description: Join Rensselaer Dining on March 18th at 3:30 pm in the Student Union Room 1602 for our Student Culinary Council Meeting. Complimentary Starbucks for the first 10 students and all participants are entered into a raffle for a FREE Jimmy John’s catering package. We hope to see you there. Be sure to follow Rensselaer Dining on social media @rensselaerdining
item.author: Students

item.title: NYS Assembly Internship Info Session
item.description: We’re excited to announce that a representative from the New York State Assembly will be visiting campus. Undergraduate and Graduate students are invited to stop by, meet our guest, and learn more about their work serving communities across New York State. The NYS Assembly Internship Program offers undergraduate and graduate students the chance to gain real‑world experience in legislative offices. Stop by on March 17 from 4-5pm in Sage 4304.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/15-3/22
item.description: Week of 3/15- 3/22: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! MAU: Sunday, 3/15 (5-7pm) at the Success Center CAL II: Monday, 3/16 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom CHEM I: Tuesday, 3/17 (5-6pm) at the Success Center CHEM II: Tuesday, 3/17 (7-8pm) at the Success Center Computer Science I: Tuesday, 3/17 (8-10pm) in the Nason Classroom Diff Eq: Wednesday, 3/18 (7-9pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Graduate Student Lunch and Learn-Easy Food Prep with SODEXO- Seating is limited. Students must register.
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 12:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Join us for Easy Food Prep with SODEXO-Lunch will be served after. Seating is limited. No walk-ins. Students must register. Email Noelle Sanchez at sanchn2@rpi.edu to RSVP.
item.author: Students

item.title: Giving Effective Presentations
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Want to present your ideas clearly and confidently in class? Want to capture your audience’s attention? This workshop will help you learn how to organize your content, design clear and effective slides, engage your audience, and deliver your message with confidence. Discover how presentations are not just about sharing information, but also about inspiring your audience.
item.author: Students

item.title: Competitions for Graduate School Salary and Tuition Info Session March 18th 10am
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM - Competitive fellowships provide a salary and tuition while you earn a graduate degree. Graduate students, seniors, and juniors: come learn how to find and apply to programs that will fund fall 2027 and beyond. Programs are available at all stages of your academic career. International students are welcome! Register for the virtual session. https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Email madige@rpi.edu for more information.
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Team Places 4th in Lockheed Martin Ethics in Engineering Case Competition!
item.description: In February two RPI undergraduate students, Nyati Misra ’27 (BMED) and John Weller ’27 (AERO), placed in the Top 4 out of 72 schools nationwide! They represented RPI with exceptional analytical rigor, professionalism, and ethical depth. Throughout the competition, they demonstrated remarkable composure, clarity of thought, and principled decision-making, serving as outstanding ambassadors for RPI. Dr. Azita Hirsa served as their faculty advisor for this competition. To learn more about the…
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 20: Carousel Digital Signage
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday March 20 from 11am-12pm, for a hands-on training session and learn how to create and publish slides on RPI’s digital signage system, Carousel Digital Signage. Whether you’re promoting a lecture, club event, or campus initiative—this session will help you get your message on screens across RPI. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Jofa’s Birthday Paw-ty
item.description: Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Join Presidential Pup Jofa to celebrate her 5th Birthday. Free Cupcakes. Stop by to pet or just say Happy Birthday!! Bring along any donation of pet food/pet product and receive free gift. All items collected will be donated for local animal needs.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cancelled - RPI Semiconductor CoLab Catalyst Seminar Series – March 2026 (Registration Required)
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 2:30 PM - 4:30 PM - The RPI Semiconductor CoLab Catalyst Seminar Series brings together faculty, researchers, and industry partners to share ideas, highlight collaborations, and advance semiconductor and microelectronics innovation. The seminar on March 17th, at the Albany NanoTech Complex, features Jacob Merson (RPI) and Dureseti (Chidu) Chidambarrao (IBM) in a two 45-min talks with a networking break. Registration is required. Follow the link for more information.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI’s Lally School of Management Launches AI Academy for Student Teams to Build and Launch Products in 30 Days
item.description: The Lally School has launched the Lally AI Academy, a 30-day AI-augmented experiential sprint that enables student teams from across campus to design, build, and launch fully functional AI-powered products. No prior coding or computer science background is required. Open to students from all five RPI schools, the Academy provides professional grade artificial intelligence tools, cloud computing infrastructure, and hands-on mentorship.
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Mercer Lecture: “Highly nonlinear optical materials and their application in photonics”
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Distinguished Mercer Lecture: “Highly nonlinear optical materials and their application in photonics” with Prof. Robert Boyd of University of Rochester. Abstract and bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Tim Mitchell (Queens College - CUNY)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Predicting Transient Behavior via Computation of Kreiss Constants” by Dr. Tim Mitchell. He is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at CUNY Queens College and Doctoral Faculty at the CUNY Graduate Center interested in numerical analysis, optimization, and scientific computing.
item.author: Community

item.title: City of Troy Street Cleaning Operations Underway
item.description: The City of Troy has commenced its seasonal street cleaning operations. Residents and visitors are strongly encouraged to observe all posted parking signs to avoid citations and vehicle towing.
item.author: Community

item.title: School of Science Presents: Diversifying Funding Sources (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Hosted by the School of Science, this session will focus on identifying and pursuing funding to support research. Presentations from OVPR, OSAT, and Foundation Philanthropy will highlight campus resources, funding tools, and strategies for securing foundation, industry, private donor, state, and federal support. Please RSVP using the provided link or contact Sibel Adali (sibel@cs.rpi.edu) for more information. Light refreshments will also be…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch – March 18, 2026
item.description: Faculty and staff are invited to enjoy a delicious lunch for just $6 plus tax on Wednesday, March 18, 2026! Join us at Commons or Russell Sage during lunch and we hope to see you there.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Recognizing January and February Staff Promotions!
item.description: Congratulations to colleagues who received promotions this January and February! Their dedication, professional growth, and ongoing contributions to RPI’s mission make a meaningful impact across our community. We are proud to recognize this important career milestone and look forward to their continued success. Log into Awardco to see who they are and join us in celebrating this well-deserved achievement!
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: TIAA Confidential Counseling (In-Person)
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM - A TIAA Financial Consultant will be available for one-on-one consultations on a broad range of retirement topics. Attend this upcoming event to help you become better informed and more confident about Rensselaer’s retirement plan benefits. Consultations will be conducted in 60 minute increments. To schedule your individual retirement counseling, please call TIAA at 1-800-732-8353 or online at https://www.tiaa.org/public/login/schedule-meeting
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty & Staff Commencement Information
item.description:
The March 20th deadline for regalia is quickly approaching! Please visit the Faculty & Staff Information page to confirm your attendance and place your regalia order. All members participating as a member of a stage party for commencement or hooding for Ph.D. are required to wear regalia.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
